reasoning: The forecast date is late October 2025, leaving just over two months until the resolution date on December 31, 2025. The community prediction just a few days prior was 2% (0.02). The resolution criteria require that at least 10 countries have committed "as a matter of treaty obligation" to restrict solar geoengineering. Formulating, negotiating, signing, and domestically ratifying an international treaty is a multi-year process. The exceptionally low community forecast strongly implies there is no such treaty nearly complete or ratified by enough states. Given the extreme lack of time left in the year, it is practically impossible for a new treaty process to reach 10 ratifications before 2026. Therefore, following the community estimate, I will predict 0.02, which is the minimum value practically applicable.
